channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A blueprint for effective cloud recovery
item.description: As businesses increasingly rely on multi-cloud environments, building cloud resilience becomes critical. In this article, we will explore the key components of cloud resilience and its importance in recovering from outages such as ransomware attacks.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Severance season 2 episode 9 recap: 7 new theories I have about 'The After Hours', and answers for Mr Bailiff, devour feculence, Svalbard, and more
item.description: Severance's latest episode has raised even more questions ahead of the Apple TV Plus show's season 2 finale.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: How Tech Leaders Are Using AI to Solve 10 Procurement Challenges
item.description: That’s why tech leaders need solutions now, not months from now. And yet, three to six months — or more — of deliberation to finalize a software purchasing decision.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intel under Tan: What enterprise IT buyers need to know
item.description: Intel’s appointment of semiconductor veteran Lip-Bu Tan as CEO marks a critical moment for the company and its enterprise customers. With rising competition from AMD, Arm-based chips, and RISC-V alternatives, Intel faces mounting pressure to defend its x86 dominance.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IT leaders are driving a new cloud computing era
item.description: At fictional Tech Innovations Inc., a midsize software company, the rapid adoption of cloud technology was once seen as a transformative stepping stone toward agility and scalability.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arbitrator sides with Meta in bid to halt sales of ex-employee's memoir
item.description: An arbitrator has sided with Meta in its case against a former employee who made a series of misconduct allegations about the Facebook and Instagram parent company in a memoir published this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Trump Administration's Drastic Cuts: Concerns Over AI-Driven Layoffs
item.description: The Trump Administration has drastically reduced the federal government workforce, sparking concerns over AI-driven layoffs and algorithmic management.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: This volunteer crew is on a mission to fix Wikipedia’s worst celebrity photos
item.description: Visit a celebrity’s Wikipedia page and there’s a good chance you’ll be greeted by a blurry, outdated, or unflattering photo. These images often look like they were snapped in passing at a public event—because, in many cases, they were.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Emily in Paris season 5: everything we know so far about the hit Netflix show’s return
item.description: Emily in Paris season 5 begins filming in May – here's everything we know about the hit Netflix show's return so far.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Samsung is offering you 8 new TVs in one bundle for March Madness, in case you want to watch all games at once like a Bond villain’s lair
item.description: In a first-of-its-kind deal, Samsung is selling you eight different TVs in one bundle so you can catch every moment of NNCA's March Madness tournament.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft faces FTC antitrust probe over AI and licensing practices
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) is reportedly pressing ahead with an antitrust investigation into Microsoft, a move that could reshape competition in AI and productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: I’m a tech pro and these are my secrets to better flights and luxury travel
item.description: Here are some travel hacks to save money on your next vacation including setting up price alerts, remaining flexible and checking for 24-hour price drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google reportedly looking at smart glasses
item.description: After abandoning its Google Glass smart glasses in 2023, Google seems ready to take another kick at the can with what will reportedly be a $115 million acquisition of Canadian startup AdHawk Microsystems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Arista delivers intelligent load balancing, AI job-centric observability
item.description: Arista Networks has added load balancing and AI job-centric observability features to core software products in an effort to help enterprise customers grow and effectively manage AI networked environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How the Pittsburgh Steelers leverage the Fortinet Security Fabric for industry-leading defense
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers are one of the most recognizable American football teams, with tens of millions of fans tuning into games and a busy stadium that can hold more than 68,000 fans.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google debuts AI model for robotics, challenging Meta, OpenAI
item.description: Research unit Google DeepMind will release Gemini Robotics, a new branch of its flagship AI model aimed at developing robots.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Malware steals bank cards and passwords from millions of devices
item.description: One report claims at least 25 million people were targeted by infostealer malware between the beginning of 2023 and the end of 2024, capturing bank card details and passwords.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 tasty new tab tricks for Chrome on Android
item.description: Quick: How many tabs do you have open in Chrome on your Android phone right now? If the answer is zero, you’re an animal. Seek immediate psychiatric help. If the answer is one to two, you’re obsessively organized and a model Android device owner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JavaOne 2025 heralds Java’s 30th birthday
item.description: With Java advancing toward its 30th anniversary, Oracle will hold its annual JavaOne conference next week, centering around the Java language, the evolution of the Java platform, and the role of Java in areas such as generative AI and machine learning.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fortinet Bolsters OT Security Platform with Enhanced Capabilities
item.description: Fortinet has upgraded its Operational Technology (OT) Security Platform to better protect industrial control systems and other operational technology networks from cyber threats. The enhanced platform includes ruggedized switches, next-generation firewalls, and improved AI capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components
item.description: Sean Plankey has been tapped to lead CISA after serving in key cyber roles during Trump's first term. His nomination comes as CISA grapples with recent cuts. The post White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ServiceNow to pay $2.85B for Moveworks’ AI tools
item.description: ServiceNow announced plans to purchase Moveworks, its front-end AI assistant and enterprise search technology for $2.85 billion in cash and stock.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding
item.description: "The Trump administration's efforts to reduce the size of the federal government have resulted in decreased funding for biomedical research," said Sandra Babosu The post Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk says X outages caused by cyberattack
item.description: Musk hypothesized on his platform that "Either a large, coordinated group and/or a country" were responsible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation
